body:
Title: Revenu Quebec Schedule B 2025 tax relief measures.

Source:
- https://www.revenuquebec.ca/en/online-services/forms-and-publications/current-details/tp-1-v/
- https://www.revenuquebec.ca/documents/en/formulaires/tp/2025-12/TP-1.D.B-V%282025-12%29.pdf

Schedule B numeric source values for encoded amounts, rates, thresholds, caps, and limits:
Value: 42090
Value: 90027
Value: 64699
Value: 2128

Value: 3470

Schedule B Part A calculates family income by adding the taxpayer's line 275 net income and the spouse's line 275 net income. Part B subtracts $42,090 from family income and floors the result at zero. The age, person living alone, and retirement income amounts are unavailable if line 18 is more than $90,027 for a taxpayer with a spouse on December 31, 2025, or more than $64,699 for a taxpayer without a spouse. Line 20 gives the person living alone amount as $2,128. Lines 22 and 23 give the age amount as $3,906 for the taxpayer and spouse if born before January 1, 1961. The retirement income work chart subtracts specified deductions and transfers from the total of lines 122 and 123, multiplies the remainder by 1.25, and caps the result at $3,470. Line 30 adds lines 20 through 28. Line 31 multiplies line 18 by 18.75%. Line 32 subtracts line 31 from line 30 and floors the result at zero. Line 34 subtracts the amount claimed on the spouse's line 361 and carries the result to TP-1 line 361.

Schedule B Part C calculates medical expenses. Line 39 multiplies family income by 3%. Line 40 subtracts line 39 from medical expenses and floors the result at zero for TP-1 line 381. Part D denies the refundable medical expense credit if family income is over $57,655 and requires work income of $3,750 or more plus the listed residency and age conditions. Line 43 adds line 40 and the disability supports deduction. Line 44 multiplies line 43 by 25% and caps the result at $1,466. Line 47 subtracts $28,335 from family income and floors the result at zero. Line 48 multiplies line 47 by 5%. Line 50 subtracts line 48 from line 44 and floors the result at zero for TP-1 line 462.

Schedule B Part E provides the independent living tax credit for seniors for a taxpayer resident in Quebec on December 31, 2025 and 70 or older on December 31, 2025. Lines 60 through 64 subtract $250 from eligible equipment or fixture expenses and floor the result at zero. Line 67 adds line 64 and functional rehabilitation transition unit expenses. Line 69 multiplies line 67 by 20% for TP-1 line 462.
